---
layout: post
title: "Automating work scrutiny"
date: 2015-02-21
---

<span></span><br />
<div style="-en-clipboard: true; text-align: center;">
<img src="data:image/png;base64," style="cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default;" /></div>
<div>
Work scrutiny is one of those jobs that can get put on the back burner and then end up never getting done if you're not careful. It can be tedious, difficult to arrange and time-consuming.</div>
<div>
And I should stress that&nbsp;I'm no big brother manager - I happen to think that most people, left to their own devices, work hard and do a good job. As a result, under <a href="http://www.mindtools.com/pages/article/newLDR_74.htm">Douglas McGregor's X/Y management theory</a> I tend toward Y - the assumption that the vast majority of people enjoy their work and want to do a good job.&nbsp;</div>
<div>
That said, I&nbsp;recognize&nbsp;the need for occasional checks and balances to ensure that there is a degree of consistency across the&nbsp;organization, that no one is struggling, and that pupils are getting the help they need. Work scrutiny is just one of the many tools at managers' disposal to ensure that things are ticking along nicely. It's an important one, but boy can it be laborious...</div>
<div>
To circumvent the worst of the logistical and&nbsp;bureaucratic&nbsp;aspects of the task I decided to automate the process as far as possible. Here's what I did:</div>
<div>
<br /></div>
<div>
I made a Google form like the one below:</div>
<div>
<br /></div>
<div>
<img src="data:image/png;base64," style="cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default;" /></div>
<div>
<br /></div>
<div>
<br /></div>
<div>
The form collects data to a Google spreadsheet to which I have added <a href="https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/autocrat/ppgnklghfnlijoafjjkpoakpjjpdkgdj">Autocrat</a>. This amazing little tool allows Google sheet users to merge the data in their sheet to a template document for sharing with anyone they specify. By asking for the initials of the&nbsp;scrutinized&nbsp;teacher in the above form I ensured that the document could be shared easy and&nbsp;automatically&nbsp;once complete (at Oswestry e-mail addresses are teacher initials, followed by a standard suffix).</div>
<div>
So, having set up the form I then sent out a&nbsp;<a href="https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nohN_2JIABBkXgYhX591zI0GePpKYzSGY3HuDIt4Qvw/edit?usp=sharing">letter like this</a>&nbsp;to a random sample of the top, middle and bottom pupils from each year group (one from each is all you need to get a flavour for what's going on). Once the books and files had been deposited on my desk I set to work.</div>
<div>
Once I had flicked through a book and filled in the form above (the work of minutes), Autocrat sent out a shared document like the sample below. The paper trail is tight - ready for when an inspector calls...!</div>
<div>
<br /></div>
<div>
<img src="data:image/png;base64," style="cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default; cursor: default;" /></div>
<br />
